_comment: Example Claude Code settings.json for a support-triage public/spawned project. Copy to settings.json and trim to the actual tool surface this project needs. The permissions.allow list is what keeps headless sessions fail-loudly instead of silently bypassing safety; scope it tightly.
_notes_for_adopters: Rename to settings.json and trim the allow list to only what this project's agents and skills actually invoke. Smaller surface = safer failures.
Never add '--dangerously-skip-permissions' to any spawn — the whole point of this file is to make permissions explicit instead.
settings.local.json is gitignored by default for user-specific overrides.
A command absent from 'allow' is already denied; the 'deny' list is belt-and-suspenders for the most dangerous flags.
